title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for an exhaustive asynchronous state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.
Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Model idle → loading → success|failure for request 3. Each state owns only valid fields, stale request 2 cannot overwrite the result, and adding "cancelled" must create an exhaustiveness diagnostic until handled.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for add, deprecate, and migrate variants with compatibility tests and consumer impact evidence.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Discriminated Unions evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Pattern Evolution: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Model idle → loading → success|failure for request 5.
